title: Ernst Chladni
body: Ernst Florens Friedrich Chladni (UK: , US: , German: […õ Ānst ňąfloňź Ā…õns ňąf Āiňźd Ā…™√ß ňąkladniňź]; 30 November 1756 ‚Äď 3 April 1827) was a German physicist and musician. His most important work, for which he is sometimes labeled the father of acoustics, included research on vibrating plates and the calculation of the speed of sound for different gases. He also undertook pioneering work in the study of meteorites and is regarded by some as the father of meteoritics.
